commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

commit.message:
Build rover device runtime, fix lamp hardware to use Robot API

## Rover (NEW — full runtime, tested on real Pi hardware)

- `devices/rover/main.py`: Entry point with --connect for WS runtime, lazy
  motion import to avoid encoder CPU starvation at startup, loads .env from
  local dir first (Pi path) then project root (laptop dev)
- `devices/rover/ws_client.py`: WebSocket client to control plane — register,
  heartbeat, auto-reconnect with backoff, Layer 1 commands via planner,
  Layer 2 spawns via agent loop in asyncio.to_thread
- `devices/rover/agent.py`: Cerebras gpt-oss-120b agent loop with 5 tools:
  move(distance_cm, speed), rotate(degrees, speed), stop(), emote(emotion),
  done(detail). tool_choice="required", warmup on boot, time budget enforcement.
  Tools map directly to tested motion.py functions.
- `devices/rover/planner.py`: Regex planner for Layer 1 direct commands —
  stop/halt/freeze, move forward/backward, turn left/right, emote keywords
- `devices/rover/motion.py`: Added 100us sleep to encoder polling threads
  to prevent 100% CPU starvation that blocked all other threads including
  LLM HTTP calls. Motors still track accurately at this poll rate.
- `devices/rover/SOUL.md`: Fleshed out rover personality — eager puppy-like
  mobile coaster, expressive through movement only, behavior guidelines
- `devices/rover/config.yaml`: Fixed ssh_user pi->rover, updated pin numbers
  to match actual motion.py hardware (IN1=23,IN2=24,IN3=16,IN4=25), updated
  actions list to match new runtime (move, rotate, stop, emote)

Tested end-to-end on real hardware:
  - Direct motor calls: move(5cm), rotate(45deg) confirmed working
  - LLM agent on Pi: warmup 408ms, tool calls <500ms, correct tool selection
  - Full master pipeline: transcript → Claude Opus dispatch → Cerebras agent
    → real motor execution → action_result back to control plane
  - Multi-device coordination: "welcome guest" dispatched to lamp + rover +
    radio simultaneously, lamp set pink glow, rover drove 30cm + excitement spin

## Lamp (hardware rewrite — Robot API replaces raw FeetechMotorsBus)

- `devices/lamp/hardware.py`: Complete rewrite to use lerobot Robot API
  (SO100FollowerConfig + make_robot_from_config + robot.send_action) instead
  of broken compat.py/FeetechMotorsBus. Smooth motion via interpolation at
  30fps with ease-in-out easing. Handles .pos suffix keys, strips gripper.
  Sim mode auto-detection.
- `devices/lamp/agent.py`: New tool set — pose(name), set_color(r,g,b),
  set_brightness(brightness), flash(r,g,b,duration_ms), pulse(r,g,b,
  cycles,period_ms), done(detail). Tools built dynamically from poses.json.
- `devices/lamp/planner.py`: Simplified to regex-only (COLOR_MAP, POSE_HINTS,
  detect_pose). Removed Cerebras planning — agent handles all decisions.
- `devices/lamp/ws_client.py`: Cleaned up Layer 1/2 routing, uses new
  hardware API and simplified planner
- `devices/lamp/main.py`: Updated for new hardware controller API
- `devices/lamp/config.yaml`: Simplified, removed stale preset values
  (poses.json is the source of truth for calibrated poses)
- `devices/lamp/compat.py`: DELETED — legacy FeetechMotorsBus wrapper,
  replaced by Robot API in hardware.py
- `devices/lamp/test_hardware.py`: New test script for hardware verification

## CLAUDE.md

- Updated lamp section with new file purposes and tool inventory
- Added rover section with full file inventory
- Updated "What's Not Built" (rover is now built)

commit.message:
Fix voice analysis never triggering after short recordings

Root cause: onTranscript was called immediately in stopRecording() using
only finalized speech results (fullTranscriptRef). For short recordings
the Speech API hasn't finalized any segments yet, so the text was empty
and the guard prevented the callback from firing.

Fix: move callback to recognition.onend which fires after all results
are finalized. Falls back to interim (displayed) text so even fast
recordings always trigger analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch from torrent to nostr strategy for P2P signaling

Nostr uses 25 relay servers vs 3 BitTorrent trackers, has a different
two-phase signaling approach (announce presence, then direct offers),
and is Trystero's default/most-tested strategy. This may fix the peer
discovery issue we've been seeing with the torrent strategy.

commit.message:
Fix 5 critical bugs: login persistence, camera, scanner UI, QR URL, reminders layout

BUG 1 - Auto logout on mobile app-switch:
  - api.js interceptor now checks token_expires_at before logging out on 401
  - If token should still be valid (future expiry), treat 401 as transient
  - Added _logoutTriggered guard to prevent racing 401s from double-clearing
  - Network errors (no response) never trigger logout

BUG 2 - Camera permission asked repeatedly:
  - Removed instance.clear() on unmount (it revokes camera access)
  - Only call instance.stop() for cleanup, preserving browser permission

BUG 3 - Scanner UI rewrite (Paytm style):
  - Full-screen edge-to-edge camera (100vw × 100vh, object-fit: cover)
  - No corner brackets — replaced with SVG mask overlay (dark edges,
    clear center rectangle with rounded corners)
  - Subtle white animated scan line sweeping through the clear area
  - Hidden all html5-qrcode default UI elements via CSS
  - Clean minimal look matching Paytm/PhonePe scanner

BUG 4 - QR URL hardcoded to aasaanapp.com:
  - qr_generator.py now reads FRONTEND_URL from os.environ at runtime
    inside create_qr_image() (not at import/module level)
  - Default fallback: aasaan-app-com-v1.vercel.app (Vercel deployment)
  - Fresh QR codes will use whatever FRONTEND_URL is set on Railway
  - Scanner extracts token from URL and navigates to /attendance?token=
    — never opens the QR URL directly in browser

BUG 5 - Payment Reminders mobile overflow:
  - Filters: grid-cols-1 on mobile, grid-cols-3 on sm+
  - Summary cards: grid-cols-3 with truncation, smaller text on mobile
  - Header: flex-col on mobile so Remind All button stacks below title
  - All text truncated to prevent overflow

commit.message:
Fix Windows file sharing violation in JsonFileRunStore

The temp-file-then-move pattern caused IOException on Windows when
SSE events triggered concurrent GET requests that held the file open
for reading during a subsequent save. Fix by opening all file handles
(read and write) with FileShare.ReadWrite so they can coexist.

Also add exception logging to fire-and-forget Task.Run calls so engine
failures are visible in the console instead of being silently swallowed.

commit.message:
fix: duplicate detection now includes dynamic blob products + type safety

Critical: findDuplicate and allocateSku now check dynamic blob products
(via getAllProductsMerged) instead of only static products.json. This
prevents duplicate SKUs when re-scanning products that were already
pushed to the blob store but not yet synced to products.json.

Also: stricter type validation on session restore, GBC-specific
PriceCharting search, type assertions for add-product merge.

commit.message:
feat(poe2): DebugPanel — raw agent output and session debug view

DebugPanel.tsx (new):
- Bottom-right overlay showing raw poe-pty-line output for a running task
- Colour-coded: green for poe: events, red for errors, grey for prose
- Node metadata bar: id, type, started time, line count
- Auto-scrolls to latest output as lines arrive
- Handover button shown when status=waiting (triggers claude --resume)
- Footer hint explains running vs waiting distinction

usePoeProject.ts:
- New poe-pty-line listener buffers last 500 raw lines per taskId in
  rawLines: Map<taskId, string[]>
- rawLines cleared on project switch
- rawLines exposed in hook return value

NodeTree.tsx:
- running nodes → onDebugOpen (DebugPanel) — view raw output

commit.message:
Split calendar sync into its own endpoint to fix timeout

The main job sync was timing out because fetching work orders for 700+
active projects (for calendar events) was added on top of the existing
ProjectionJob sync, exceeding the 5-minute limit.

- Restore main sync to ProjectionJobs only (fast, proven working)
- New POST /api/coperniq/calendar-sync handles calendar events separately
  with its own 5-minute budget — scans all active projects regardless of phase,
  only syncs SOLAR_INSTALL / BATTERY_INSTALL / INSPECTION / SERVICE visits
  from today forward
- Add "Sync Calendar" button (orange) to the Coperniq Sync page with its
  own loading state, result display, and error handling

feat: internalize combined narrative into FlowChartExecutor

enableNarrative() now auto-attaches a NarrativeRecorder to ScopeFacade-based
scopes, so getNarrative() returns a combined narrative (flow + data operations)
without the user needing to manually create and wire up recorders.

New methods:
- getNarrative(): combined narrative (flow + data) when ScopeFacade is used
- getFlowNarrative(): flow-only sentences (previous behavior)
- getNarrativeEntries(): structured CombinedNarrativeEntry[] for programmatic use

Bumps version to 0.2.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate frame-freeze spikes from shoot() and per-frame allocations

Root cause: shoot() called ray.intersectObjects(scene.children, false)
which after building detail additions contains 4000+ individual mesh
objects (each trim piece, window frame, glass pane added to scene
separately). A shotgun shot (8 pellets) triggered 32000+ raycast tests
per frame, causing a hard stall.

Fix: Replace scene.children with environmentMeshes[] — a curated array
populated only with bullet-hittable geometry (terrain, exterior wall
panels, rocks, tree trunks). Now ~300 objects instead of 4000+.

Also eliminated all per-frame heap allocations:
- shoot(): pre-allocated _shootRay + _shootDir replaces new Raycaster
  + new Vector3 per pellet; _pieceMeshScratch + _aliveBotMeshScratch
  replace .map()/.filter() array allocations per shot; placed-piece
  lookup simplified from traverse to direct === comparison
- updatePlayer(): _moveFwd/_moveRight/_moveVec/_nextPos replace 4 new
  Vector3 allocations every frame
- runBotShooting(): _botFromPos replaces new Vector3 per bot shot;
  camera.position passed directly (spawnTracer copies values)

refactor: unified interactive chooser with create option

opencapy (no args) and opencapy ls now both invoke runChooser(), which
shows a '[+] new  <dirname>' option at the top alongside existing sessions.
Selecting it creates and attaches a new session for the current directory.

- fzf: new entry uses a null-byte sentinel key (\x00new) so it's
  unambiguous; --with-nth=2 hides the key column from display.
- numbered: 'n' or 'new' input triggers creation; number/name attaches.
- Removes the special-cased 'inside tmux → tmux choose-session' path
  since that UI can't create sessions; our chooser works everywhere.
- opencapy <name> remains attach-only with a clear error on miss.
- opencapy new [name] remains the non-interactive creation path.

feat: Round 2 EAS experiments complete - all 24 baselines tested

- Implemented 5 fixed baselines: DynMM, ADMN, Centaur, TFN, FDSNet
- Implemented 3 NAS baselines: DARTS, LLMatic, EvoPrompting
- Fixed MOSEI data preprocessing (continuous -> 10-class classification)
- Tested all 24 experiments (8 methods × 3 datasets)
- VQA: 3129 classes, EAS achieves 52.4% vs baseline ~0%
- IEMOCAP: 9 classes, EAS achieves 52.1% vs baseline ~10%
- MOSEI: 10 classes, EAS achieves 49.6% vs baseline ~22%

Key findings:
- EAS shows 2.2x improvement on MOSEI
- EAS shows 5x improvement on IEMOCAP
- EAS achieves absolute dominance on VQA (extreme sparsity)

New files:
- experiments/run_baseline.py: unified baseline evaluation
- src/baselines/*: 8 baseline fusion modules
- configs/round2_eas_*.yaml: experiment configs
- docs/AI_CONTEXT.md: experiment tracking
- docs/ROUND2_EXPERIMENT_*.md: design and results
